channel.title: Sleep on VitaminDWiki
channel.description: Recent content in Sleep on VitaminDWiki

item.title: Rotating shift workers have 3X higher risk of sleep problems if low Vitamin D
item.description:
Association of Hypovitaminosis D with Sleep Parameters in Rotating Shift Worker Drivers

Sleep Sci 2023; 16(01): 084-091 DOI: 10.1055/s-0043-1767748

Luiz Antônio Alves de Menezes-Júnior , Virgínia Capistrano Fajardo , Raimundo Marques do

Nascimento Neto , Sílvia Nascimento de Freitas , Fernando Luiz Pereira Oliveira , Fausto Aloísio Pedrosa Pimenta , George Luiz Lins Machado-Coelho , Adriana Lúcia Meireles

Objective To evaluate the association between sleep parameters and hypovitaminosis D in rotating shift drivers.

The role of interaction between vitamin D and VDR FokI gene polymorphism (rs2228570) in sleep quality of adults

Sci Rep. 2024 Apr 7;14(1):8141. doi: 10.1038/s41598-024-58561-2.

Luiz Antônio Alves de Menezes-Júnior 1 2 3, Thais da Silva Sabião 4 5, Samara Silva de Moura 4 5, Aline Priscila Batista 6 7, Mariana Carvalho de Menezes 4 5 8, Júlia Cristina Cardoso Carraro 4 5 8, George Luiz Lins Machado-Coelho 4 6 7, Adriana Lúcia Meireles 4 5 8

item.title: Psoriasis increases risk of Obstructive Sleep Apnea by 1.4X (vitamin D not mentioned)
item.description:
Analysis supports link between psoriasis and obstructive sleep apnea

MD Edge

“Patients with psoriasis had a 1.77-fold increased risk of having obstructive sleep apnea”

  • After adjust forage, gender, race, BMI, smoking status, type 2 diabetes, congestive heart failure, hypertension, history of myocardial infarction, angina, and peripheral artery disease (OR, 1.45 , 95% CI, 1.35 - 1.55; P <.001).

Both Psoriasis and Sleep Apnea are associated with low vitamin D

VitaminDWiki – Sleep Apnea and Vitamin D - many studies 13+

VitaminDWiki – Diseases that may be related via low vitamin D

The >80 associations includes:

Sleep Apnea and Vitamin D Deficiency - Mercola interview Gominak Feb 2024

86 minutes dual, connected epidemics: Low D, Sleep Apnea

item.title: Sleep duration increases with vitamin D levels in males, broad peak in females
item.description:
• The saturation effect of 25(OH)D level on sleep duration for older people:The NHANES 2011–2018

The saturation effect of 25(OH)D level on sleep duration for older people:The NHANES 2011–2018

Geriatric Nursing Volume 53, September–October 2023, Pages 198-203 https://doi.org/10.1016/j.gerinurse.2023.07.008   PDF is behind a paywall

Ran Duan MSN a b 1, Tong Feng MD c d 1, JiXin Hou BSN b, Yu Wei BSN b, Lin Lin PhD e

This study aimed to investigate the potential relationship between vitamin D and sleep duration in older adults. The study utilized multivariate linear regression models to estimate the associations between serum 25(OH)D and sleep duration. In addition, a smooth curve fitting approach was used to identify any non-linear trends between the two variables. The study included 15,749 participants over the age of 60. The results showed a positive correlation between serum 25(OH)D levels and sleep duration in the fully-adjusted model. This correlation was observed in both males and females, as well as in non-Hispanic White and non-Hispanic Black participants. No significant interactions were found between serum 25(OH)D levels and the stratifying variables. The curve fitting analysis revealed a non-linear relationship between 25(OH)D and sleep duration, with a saturation point observed at a serum 25(OH)D level of 40.6 ng/mL. In conclusion, the findings suggest that there is a positive correlation between serum 25(OH)D levels and sleep duration, with a saturation effect observed. A positive correlation is evident when serum 25(OH)D falls below 40.6 ng/mL.

item.title: Sleep greatly improved by 50,000 IU of vitamin D once every two weeks – RCT
item.description:
• The effect of vitamin D supplement on the score and quality of sleep in 20-50 year-old people with sleep disorders compared with control group

The effect of vitamin D supplement on the score and quality of sleep in 20-50 year-old people with sleep disorders compared with control group

Nutr Neurosci . 2018 Sep;21(7):511-519. doi: 10.1080/1028415X.2017.1317395.

Mohammad Shahi Majid 1, Hosseini Seyed Ahmad 1, Helli Bizhan 1, Haghighi Zade Mohammad Hosein 2, Abolfathi Mohammad 3

item.title: Sleep quality 6X worse after stroke if low vitamin D

Front Nutr. 2022 Jul 22 doi: 10.3389/fnut.2022.937867

Guiqian Huang 1 , Jiahao Chen 1 , Luqian Zhan 1 , Jingfang Long 1 , Yisi Lin 1 , Beilei Zhu 1 , Jincai He 1

Click here for more information

item.title: Poor sleep associated with low Vitamin D (pregnant Saudis in the case)
item.description:
• Changes in Sleep Patterns during Pregnancy and Predictive Factors: A Longitudinal Study in Saudi Women

Changes in Sleep Patterns during Pregnancy and Predictive Factors: A Longitudinal Study in Saudi Women

Nutrients. 2022 Jun 25;14(13):2633. doi: 10.3390/nu14132633.

Sara Al-Musharaf 1

This study aimed to assess sleep patterns during the three trimesters of pregnancy and whether vitamin D concentrations, along with other risk factors, are associated with these alterations. In a longitudinal study, 140 pregnant women (age 18 to 39 years) were followed throughout their first, second, and third trimesters. Sleep was measured using the Pittsburgh Sleep Quality Index (PSQI) at each trimester, along with an assessment of biochemical parameters, including serum vitamin D levels. The information that was collected included anthropometric data, socio-economic status, dietary intake, and physical activity. The PSQI was higher in mid and late pregnancy than in early pregnancy (both p = 0.001), and the sleep duration was also higher in late versus early pregnancy.

item.title: Sleep improved a bit by adding a bit of vitamin D – meta-anaysis
item.description:
• Vitamin D Supplementation and Sleep: A Systematic Review and Meta-Analysis of Intervention Studies

Vitamin D Supplementation and Sleep: A Systematic Review and Meta-Analysis of Intervention Studies

Nutrients 2022, 14(5), 1076; https://doi.org/10.3390/nu14051076

by Myriam Abboud. Department of Health Sciences, College of Natural and Health Sciences, Zayed University, Dubai, United Arab Emirates

Background: Vitamin D deficiency is associated with sleep disorders and poor sleep quality. Whether vitamin D supplementation (VDS) helps resolve these problems remains unclear. Objective: To systematically review the effect of VDS on sleep quantity, quality, and disorders, and perform a meta-analysis of available data.

item.title: Many sleep problems are associated with low Vitamin D (children in this case)

J. Mol. Sci. 2022,23, 1430.https://doi.org/10.3390/ijms23031430

Federica Prono 1, Katerina Bernardi1, Raffaele Ferri 2 and Oliviero Bruni 3 0

00185 Rome, Italy; federica.prono@uniroma1.it (F.P.); katerina.bernardi@uniroma1.it (K.B.)

Sleep Research Centre, Department of Neurology IC, Oasi Research Institute—IRCCS, 94108 Troina, Italy; rferri@oasi.en.it

THE EFFECTS OF VITAMIN D SUPPLEMENTATION ON PROGNOSIS IN PATIENTS WITH MILD OBSTRUCTIVE SLEEP APNEA SYNDROME

Turk J Med Sci. 2021 Jun 27. doi: 10.3906/sag-2101-83

Feride Ayyildiz, Hilal Yildiran, Nigar Afandiyeva, Özlem Gülbahar, Oğuz Köktürk

Download the PDF from VitaminDWiki

Sleep Apnea Events

Background/aim: The aim of this study was to investigate the effect of vitamin D on the disease prognosis and biochemical parameters in patients with mild obstructive sleep apnea syndrome(OSAS).

Grinding teeth while sleeping (sleep bruxism) 6 X more likely if low vitamin D
item.description:
Self-reported sleep bruxism is associated with vitamin D deficiency and low dietary calcium intake: a case-control study

BMC Oral Health. 2021 Jan 7;21(1):21. doi: 10.1186/s12903-020-01349-3.

Mohammad J Alkhatatbeh 1, Zainab L Hmoud 2, Khalid K Abdul-Razzak 3, Esam M Alem 4

Download the PDF from VitaminDWiki

Background: Sleep bruxism may result in deleterious effects including loss of tooth enamel, fracture of teeth or restorations, teeth hypersensitivity or pain, and headache. The aim was to study the link between sleep bruxism, low serum vitamin D, low consumption of dietary calcium, psychological symptoms, and frequent headache.

Sarayloo F1,2, Dionne-Laporte A2, Catoire H2, Rochefort D2, Houle G1,2, Ross JP1,2, Akçimen F1,2, Barros Oliveira R2, Turecki G1,3, Dion PA2,4, Rouleau GA2,4.

    Deactivation of Iron gene: HMOX1

item.title: The story of the Human Body -excellent book by Daniel Lieberman
item.description:
The Story of the Human Body: Evolution, Health, and Disease - 2014

Review and notes by Henry Lahore, founder of VitaminDWiki

  • Book provided me with great insight into how, during a few decades, the immune system genes (Vitamin D Receptor, etc) could have radically changed.

  • People with poor immune systems in the 1800’s 20% died before becoming parents.

  • Instead of survival of the fittest (80% survived) we now have survival of the survivors (99% survive)

PLOS x July 5, 2019https://doi.org/10.1371/journal.pone.0219318

Ezgi Dogan-Sander , Anja Willenberg, İnci Batmaz, Cornelia Enzenbach, Kerstin Wirkner, Elisabeth Kohls, Roland Mergl, Joachim Thiery, Jürgen Kratzsch, Ulrich Hegerl, Christian Sander

Note

  1. 60% of potential study participants were rejected. Example reasons: shift workers , had serious health problems, failed to complete data for 5 sequential nights

  2. 44% of participants were retired - more likely to have consistant bedtime (weekdays and weekends)

item.title: Sleep, Vitamin D, B Vitamins, jaw, etc. Dr. Gominak transcript - 2016
item.description:
Interview on YouTube

Transcript is down this page (transcript can be read in 102+ languages)

Nice transcript by Scribie.com $0.83 / minute

Download the Audio file

Download the un-highlighted transcript text

Key Takeaways (from YouTube)

02:29 Hundreds of Dr. Gominak’s patients, children, teenagers and young healthy females, didn’t have REM sleep.

04:42 Sleep apnea, fibromyalgia, chronic fatigue were not recognized in the 1960s and 1970s, because they were not epidemic.

item.description:
A cross-syndrome cohort comparison of sleep disturbance in children with Smith-Magenis syndrome, Angelman syndrome, autism spectrum disorder and tuberous sclerosis complex.

J Neurodev Disord. 2018 Mar 1;10(1):9. doi: 10.1186/s11689-018-9226-0.

Trickett J1,2, Heald M3, Oliver C3, Richards C3.

1 Department of Health Sciences, College of Life Sciences, George Davies Centre, University of Leicester, Leicester, LE1 7RH, UK. jkt12@leicester.ac.uk.

2 Cerebra Centre for Neurodevelopmental Disorders, School of Psychology, University of Birmingham, Birmingham, B15 2TT, UK. jkt12@leicester.ac.uk.
